stance.note: Re-added as RandomForest so the (Upr, Stn) lookup table can use ML-driven Stance on Render. Rule-based stance still wins LOOCV but ML pushes more clips into the unanimous lookup cells, which matters more for downstream Confidence accuracy.

confidence.note: Downgraded from StackingClassifier to RandomForest because StackingClassifier pickles do not deserialize cleanly across the local/Render numpy/sklearn split. RF is slightly less accurate in LOOCV but actually loads on the report-worker.

authority.note: ML model intentionally omitted. In-sample ML stacking inverted L/H predictions on most clips (5/28 correct). Ground truth analysis shows Confidence == Authority in 26/28 clips (93%), so Authority is derived from the blended-hybrid Confidence band via score_authority(), which applies the Andre-pattern downgrade for polished-posture-no-drive clips (distinct<=5 AND hand_block<=0.10). Result: 10/28 exact (vs 5/28 with ML), 13/28 adjacent (vs 5/28).
